rulings.Case Title: SAMUEL CARL SCHONER VS. JANET MCKINLEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, June 11, 2025, line 1, PETITIONER SAMUEL SCHONER'S Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award The unopposed motion to confirm arbitration award is granted. Petitioner shall submit a proposed judgment in conformity with the arbitration award. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/CVA)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Plaintiffs Mental Health Records And Deposition Testimony Re Said Records From Karyn Maag-Weigand
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, June 11, 2025, line 3, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S Motion To Compel Production Of Plaintiffs Mental Health Records And Deposition Testimony Re Said Records From Karyn Maag-Weigand The District seeks to compel Jane Doe's complete mental health records from her treating psychologist, Karyn Maag-Weigand. Jane Doe invokes the patient-psychotherapist privilege and objects to the scope of these records. rulings.Case Title: CREDIT CORP SOLUTIONS INC., VS. JASON JONES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss With Prejudice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, June 11, 2025, line 8, DEFENDANT JASON JONES' Motion To Dismiss With Prejudice Defendant's motion to dismiss is denied. Defendant fails to cite a statutory or legal basis for this motion. Defendant argues that plaintiff's claims lack merit, it has abused the discovery process, and it violated federal law. A party seeking a dismissal because a claim lacks evidentiary merit must comply with CCP 437c. A party seeking a dismissal because of discovery abuses must comply with CCP 2023.010-2023.040. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DILLON JOHNSTONE VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Dillon Johnstone'S Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, June 11, 2025, line 9, DEFENDANT TUSHAR TAMBE'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff Dillon Johnstone'S Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One Defendant Tushar Tambe's unopposed motion to compel further responses to his requests for production of documents, set one, from plaintiff Dillon Johnstone and for sanctions is granted in part. Defendant shows good cause for production. Johnstone is ordered to provide further responses to RFP Nos. 1, 3, 4, 5, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 18, 19, 20, 30, 32. All responsive documents shall be produced within 20 days of entry of this order. Some of Johnstone's RFP responses suggest he no longer seeks recovery for lost earnings. If that is the case, he must make it clear. For the above-listed RFPs plus RFP No. 2, Johnstone shall produce all responsive documents within 20 days of entry of this order. For the following RFPs, Johnstone shall amend his response to confirm he has conducted a reasonably diligent search: 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 29, 31. For the following responses, Johnstone's objections have merit or his responses are complete; no further response is ordered: 16, 17. Tambe seeks sanctions. CCP 2031.300(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to a demand for inspection . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." Sanctions may be awarded even where the nonmoving party does not oppose the motion. (Rule of Court 3.1348(a).) Tambe's sanctions request is reasonable and the court does not find substantial justification. The court orders Johnstone to pay $1290 to Tambe within 20 days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/CVA)
